source.definition: to provide a note, explanation, or opinion about a text or subject

explanation.sourceText: The English verb "comment" comes from the French word "commenter", which itself derives from the Latin "commentarius" meaning a note or explanation.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „comment” provine din francezul „commenter”, care la rândul său derivă din latinul „commentarius”, însemnând o notă sau explicație.

source.additional: The verb is frequently used on social media to share opinions or add information.

target.definition: a face o observație sau explicație despre un text sau un subiect

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian verb „a comenta” was borrowed from the French „commenter”, which in turn comes from the Latin „commentari”.
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a comenta” este împrumutat din francezul „commenter”, derivat la rândul său din latinul „commentari”.

target.additional: „A comenta“ is often used in journalistic, academic, and social discussions.

explanation.sourceText: Providing an explanation of a text or topic.
explanation.targetText: Explicarea unui text sau a unui subiect.

examples.sourceText: I explain the text for my readers.

explanation.sourceText: The act of expressing an opinion about a topic.
explanation.targetText: Actul de a exprima o opinie despre un subiect.

examples.sourceText: I express my opinion on the topic.
Do you comment on the video?
Do you (plural) comment on the posts?
I have commented on the post.
He commented on the importance of the topic.
Tomorrow I will comment on the article.

learningTips.text: The Romanian verb "a comenta" sounds very similar to the English "comment", both meaning to give an opinion or explanation.
Remember "a comenta" by thinking of the English word "comment" which contains "men"—just like "opinion"—helping you recall its meaning.
Imagine you are adding your thoughts to a text by "commenting" on it; this makes "a comenta" easy to remember.
Think of online platforms where you "comment" on posts—this everyday use links directly to the Romanian "a comenta".
